---
title: "Food Allergens in India: A Synthesis"
subtitle: "Prevalence, Evidence Base, and a Defensible Allergen List"
author: "iSRL"
date: "2026-03-31"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
  pdf:
    toc: true
status: "in-progress — structure tentative"
---

> **Note on structure:** This manuscript is built incrementally as papers are read. Section headings are placeholders; they will be revised or collapsed as evidence shapes the argument. Do not treat the current structure as final.

---

# 1. What is a food allergen?

<!-- PLACEHOLDER — to be written after reading P01, P05, P06 -->
<!-- Key questions to answer here:
     - What biological mechanism distinguishes an allergen from a toxic food compound?
     - What is the difference between sensitisation (the immune system building a response) and clinical reactivity (symptoms when exposed)?
     - What protein families are involved?
     - Why do some foods cause more allergic reactions than others? -->

*Section not yet written. Awaiting paper extraction.*

---

# 2. How food allergy is measured and how it is declared

<!-- PLACEHOLDER — to be written after reading P01, P04, P06 -->
<!-- Key questions:
     - What are the main ways to test for food allergy? (skin prick test, blood IgE test, oral food challenge — what each measures and where they disagree)
     - What is the difference between a regulatory declaration requirement and a clinical threshold?
     - What does FSSAI currently require to be declared and on what legal basis?
     - What international frameworks exist (Big 8/9 US, Big 14 EU, FSANZ, Codex) and how do they differ structurally? -->

*Section not yet written. Awaiting paper extraction.*

---

# 3. Food allergens in India: what the literature says

<!-- PLACEHOLDER — core synthesis section, built from all 10 papers -->
<!-- Structure TBD — likely:
     3.1 Allergens with strong evidence (multiple studies, defined prevalence)
     3.2 Allergens with weak or single-study evidence
     3.3 What the literature does not cover — documented gaps
     3.4 Challenges recorded by authors themselves -->

*Section not yet written. Awaiting paper extraction.*

---

# 4. The FSSAI list of 8 allergens: convergence and divergence with evidence

<!-- PLACEHOLDER — comparative section -->
<!-- The FSSAI list (to be confirmed from source):
     Reportedly: cereals with gluten, crustaceans, eggs, fish, peanuts, soybeans, milk, tree nuts
     — DON'T KNOW: exact current FSSAI list text and legal citation -->

*Section not yet written. FSSAI list to be confirmed from primary regulation.*

---

# 5. A defensible allergen list for India

<!-- PLACEHOLDER — synthesis output -->
<!-- This section will build the list with explicit criteria:
     - Why each allergen is included (evidence level, mechanism, population data)
     - Why some are excluded or flagged as uncertain
     - What "defensible" means operationally here -->

*Section not yet written. Depends on §3 and §4.*

---

# References

<!-- To be populated from allergen.papertable.qmd as papers are read -->
